data.title: ASML options price 6% earnings move as traders favor upside
data.description:
Options traders are positioning for a sizable move in ASML (ASML) shares following the semiconductor equipment maker’s first-quarter 2026 earnings report, scheduled for early Wednesday.

As of Tuesday's close, ASML’s (ASML) April 17 weekly options chain suggests the market is pricing in a post-earnings move of about 6.3%, translating to a market-implied trading range of roughly $1,420 to $1,610. With the stock trading around $1,518 a share, straddles at nearby strikes were valued at $95 to $97.

What stands out in the options activity is heavy trading in the belly of the chain on both sides—typically a sign of event-volatility positioning rather than simple directional bets. The $1,500 calls saw 542 contracts trade against 1,454 open interest, while 1,520 calls traded 586 contracts against just 273 open interest.

The wing activity tells an interesting story: traders appear more willing to chase upside than position for disaster.

Call volume was notably heavy at strikes well above spot, including the $1,600 and $1,700 levels. Downside protection was present but concentrated at lower strikes like $1,400 and $1,440, suggesting traders want some insurance but aren’t betting on a collapse near current levels.

data.title: NN sees Q1 beat, lifts 2026 new business outlook to $80–90M range
data.description:
• NN (NNBR) announced on Tuesday that its preliminary Q1 2026 net sales are expected to show YoY growth and come in ahead of its internal forecast.
  • The company's new business program delivered strong Q1 results and secured a ~$43M peak annual sales award, driven by electric grid and data center demand.
  • Following this, the company has raised its full-year 2026 guidance to $80M–$90M, reflecting continued strength into early Q2.
  • Moreover, more than 60 new programs were launched, creating a shippable backlog, as orders exceeded production during the quarter.
  • NN (NNBR) kept its $445M–$465M net sales guidance, now expected in the upper half, while shifting toward higher non-auto business.
  • CEO Harold Bevis said, We are currently ahead of our 2026 and long-term targets and will share more details in the Q1 2026 earnings release scheduled for May 6, 2026, as the company is still finalizing final results.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: OpenAI releases new Cyber model one week after Anthropic's Mythos Preview made waves
data.description:
OpenAI (OPENAI) revealed its GPT-5.4-Cyber today, exactly one week after Anthropic (ANTHRO) made waves with the public revelation of its Claude Mythos Preview, which also featured advanced cybersecurity capabilities.

The advent of Mythos prompted a range of cybersecurity stocks to fall. However, these were mostly static during early post-market trading on Tuesday.

Similar to Anthropic's release of Mythos, which was only shared with about 40 big tech companies, OpenAI is also being selective with who gains access to Cyber. The model will be available for some members of OpenAI's Trusted Access for Cyber program. Individual users and enterprises can apply to join the program.

"All customers approved through this process will gain access to versions of existing models with reduced friction around safeguards which might trigger on dual-use cyber activity, allowing them to continue to support security education, defensive programming, and responsible vulnerability research," OpenAI said.

While AI tools pose greater threats to software systems, they can also help identify threats and vulnerabilities. OpenAI plans to allow more users to test Cyber's capabilities in the weeks and months ahead.

"Digital infrastructure has already been vulnerable for years, before advanced AI even came along," OpenAI said. "Now, existing models can help find vulnerabilities, reason across codebases, and support meaningful parts of the cyber workflow, and threat actors are experimenting with novel AI-driven approaches. We've seen sophisticated harnesses elicit stronger and stronger capabilities by using more test-time compute with existing models. That means safeguards cannot wait for a single future threshold."

data.title: AAR secures $305M contract from U.S. Navy
data.description:
• AAR (AIR) announced on Tuesday the company was awarded an approximately $305M contract to provide contractor logistics support for the United States Navy and Marine Corps C-40A fleet.
  • AAR's support will ensure the aircraft's continued operational readiness and long‑term sustainment, reinforcing its ability to meet mission requirements, the company said. 
  • It added that the firm-fixed-price indefinite-delivery/indefinite-quantity contract includes main operating base logistics and material support; field team and detachment support; scheduled and unscheduled depot-level aircraft and component maintenance, modification, and repair; commercial line maintenance; and scheduled and unscheduled organizational-level and depot-level support equipment maintenance, modification, and repair.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Nike gets lift from insider buys from CEO Hill, Tim Cook
data.description:
• Nike (NKE) shares saw a modest bump in Tuesday’s after-hours trading after CEO Elliott Hill and Apple (AAPL) CEO Tim Cook increased their stakes in the sportswear giant.
  • According to a filing with the U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission, Cook, who has been on the Nike (NKE) board since November 2005, purchased 25K shares last week at an average price of $42.43 per share. This brings Cook’s total holdings in Nike (NKE) to 130,480 shares.
  • On Monday, Nike (NKE) CEO Elliott Hill bought ~24K shares at an average price of $42.265 per share, increasing his total holdings to 265,248 shares.

data.title: GitLab jumps amid Google Cloud collaboration expansion
data.description:
• GitLab (GTLB) shares jumped more than 7% in extended trading on Tuesday after the company disclosed it would be expanding its collaboration with Google Cloud (GOOG) (GOOGL).
  • As part of the enlarged deal, Google Cloud customers will be able to use the GitLab Duo Agent Platform with Google's Vertex AI models; that usage will go towards their existing Google Cloud commitments, GitLab said in a statement.
  • “AI agents are only as good as the context they operate on and the governance around them,” said Manav Khurana, chief product and marketing officer, GitLab, in the release. “GitLab is where that context lives across issues, code, pipelines, security findings, and this partnership connects it to Vertex AI's strongest models. As agents take on more of the software lifecycle, the platform that provides both the context and the controls becomes the critical layer.”

data.title: 3 things to look out for on Wednesday
data.description:
U.S. stocks ended comfortably in the green on Tuesday as investors grew optimistic about President Donald Trump’s announcement that negotiations with Iran could take place “over the next two days” in Pakistan.

The benchmark S&P 500 (SP500) closed +1.2%, while the Nasdaq Composite (COMP:IND) ended +2%, and the blue-chip Dow (DJI) finished +0.7%.

The bond market was lower. The benchmark 10-year Treasury yield (US10Y) was 4 basis points lower at 4.25%, while the 2-year Treasury yield (US2Y) dropped 3 basis points to 3.75%.

Crude oil futures (CL1:COM) were lower at $92.20, while Brent (CO1:COM) was at $95.16 per barrel.

Now, here are three things to look out for on Wednesday:

  • Wednesday is an important day for earnings, as bank giants JPMorgan Chase & Co. (JPM), Wells Fargo (WFC), Citigroup (C) are set to report earnings in the pre-market hours. Other big companies reporting earnings include Johnson & Johnson (JNJ), BlackRock (BLK), Albertsons Cos. (ACI), CarMax (KMX), and Equity Bancshares (EQBK), among others.
  • The geopolitical landscape remains volatile as President Donald Trump indicated that Iran had reached out for potential negotiations, even as the U.S. moved forward with a naval blockade of the Strait of Hormuz. Then, Trump on Tuesday hinted that negotiations with Iran could take place over the next two days in Pakistan. Meanwhile, oil prices have declined by around 5% in the past five days. Crude oil futures (CL1:COM) were lower on Tuesday at $92.17, while Brent (CO1:COM) was at $95.25 per barrel. Investors will continue to monitor oil prices.
  • Lastly, Trump’s administration is planning to launch a system to start issuing refunds to American importers for the $166B companies paid in tariffs, as the U.S. Supreme Court struck down the tariff agenda as unlawful. According to the U.S. Customs and Border Protection, it has finished the development of the initial phase of a refund system, which will consolidate refunds to importers via electronic payments. Traders will be on the lookout for how this can affect the markets.

data.title: HeartBeam announces stock offering; shares drop 18% after-hours
data.description:
• HeartBeam (BEAT) announced on Tuesday that it has commenced an underwritten public offering of its common stock, with all shares being issued and sold by the company, subject to market conditions.
  • The net proceeds will be used to launch and grow its FDA-approved ECG system, plus develop its extended-wear patch, support heart attack detection initiatives, and enhance AI capabilities.
  • The remaining proceeds will be used for working capital and general corporate purposes. Moreover, Titan Partners is serving as sole bookrunner for the offering.
  • HeartBeam (BEAT) closed flat at $1.20 but slipped to $0.98 in after-hours trading, down ~18.33%.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Equity Bancshares GAAP EPS of $0.80 misses by $0.14, revenue of $64.1M misses by $18.57M
data.description:
• Equity Bancshares press release (EQBK): Q1 GAAP EPS of $0.80 misses by $0.14.
  • Revenue of $64.1M (+5.7% Y/Y) misses by $18.57M.

  • Loans held for investment were $5.4 billion at period end, increasing $1.2 billion during the quarter. Total assets closed the quarter at $7.7 billion, a $1.3 billion increase from prior quarter end.

    Total deposit balances closed the quarter at $6.3 billion increasing $1.2 billion from the previous quarter end. Brokered deposits closed the quarter at 5.7% of total deposits up from 1.4% at prior quarter end.

data.title: Kinetik GAAP EPS of $2.16, revenue of $430.4M
data.description:
• Kinetik press release (KNTK): Q4 GAAP EPS of $2.16.
  • Revenue of $430.4M.

  • Kinetik reported net income including noncontrolling interest of $416.7 million and $525.9 million for the three and twelve months ended December 31, 2025, respectively. Kinetik generated Adjusted EBITDA1 of $252.1 million and $987.7 million, Distributable Cash Flow1 of $151.7 million and $620.5 million, and Free Cash Flow1 of $(12.0) million and $167.2 million for the three and twelve months ended December 31, 2025, respectively.
